summary:
The issue is clear: after `InstallSnapshot`, the `commitIndex` is not updated to match the snapshot's `lastIncludedIndex`. This causes `Restart()` to set `lastApplied` to the old `commitIndex` (0), which is less than the snapshot boundary (10). When new entries are replicated, entries 1-10 get reapplied.

The fix is in `snapshot.go`: update `commitIndex` in `InstallSnapshot`.

```bash
cd /app/app && go build -o /usr/local/bin/raft-snapshot . 2>&1
```

```bash
cd /app && ./test.sh
```
